raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I paid {$270.00} to a property in XXXX, XXXX, booked through Booking.com. I later filed a dispute with Citibank for " services not as described '' because the apartment materially differed from the listing, including unsafe conditions, missing amenities, misrepresented photos, and incorrect address provided at the time of the stay. 

Before contacting Citibank, I attempted to resolve the issue through XXXX. Booking confirmed that the owner falsely cancelled the reservation after the fact but stated that they were unable to issue a refund because we paid the property directly. They acknowledged that the merchant was unresponsive, but refused to assist further. Booking later removed the property listing from their website. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I filed the dispute with Citibank, and they immediately issued a conditional credit to my account. On XX/XX/XXXX, Citibank resolved the dispute, stating " The services or merchandise were provided, therefore, we're unable to support the billing dispute, '' effectively siding with the merchant. On XX/XX/XXXX, I called Citibank to request that the dispute be re-opened due to misclassification of the dispute category, since I was not contesting that the stay took place, but rather that the property materially differed from what was advertised on XXXX. On XX/XX/XXXX, Citibank denied my request to re-open the dispute, stating : " We have reviewed the information you provided regarding your dispute. Without a valid credit slip, we are unable to intervene on your behalf with the merchant or their bank. If you are unable to obtain this, you will need to seek other means to obtain credit. '' After receiving Citibank 's initial response, I requested confirmation that my supporting documents had been received and that the dispute be reconsidered. Citibank confirmed the receipt of the documents but still denied the dispute and refused to re-open it. Their statement that a " credit slip '' is required is incorrect and illogical, because no refund has ever been processed or issued by the merchant. The merchant has refused to cooperate at every step of the process, and therefore no credit exists that could generate a credit slip. I am requesting that Citibank properly review this dispute under the correct category and either reinstate the credit or provide a clear explanation of why a credit slip is required in a case where no refund has been issued.
